---
title: "Research"
pagetitle: "Research"
page-layout: article
comments: false
sidebar: false
---

My research lies at the intersection of formal semantics and syntax. I am particularly interested in how modal meaning relates to the syntactic properties of complements.

::: {.legend-box}

**Filter:**

<span class="legend-item" data-topic="topic-semantics"><span class="legend-color topic-semantics"></span>Semantics</span>

<span class="legend-item" data-topic="topic-syntax"><span class="legend-color topic-syntax"></span>Syntax</span>

<span class="legend-item" data-topic="topic-experimental"><span class="legend-color topic-processing"></span>Experiments</span>

:::

<script src="/files/filter-v1.js"></script>

::: {.research-topic .topic-semantics .topic-syntax}

## Modality and complement structure

I investigate the relationship between modal flavor and the syntactic properties of complements. I am especially interested in how epistemic and non-epistemic interpretations interact with complement structure.

My broader goal is to understand how syntactic structure constrains the interpretation of modal expressions.

:::

::: {.research-topic .topic-semantics .topic-syntax .topic-processing}

## Modal ellipsis and anaphora

My current work examines modal ellipsis and anaphora in French. I use these phenomena to investigate the relationship between modal flavor, complement structure, and the interpretation of unpronounced material.

My approach is primarily theoretical, complemented by experimental work.

### Selected work

<div class="ref-item topic-semantics topic-syntax topic-processing">
<span class="ref-text">
<strong>Chamot, V.</strong> (2026). <em>French Modal Ellipsis and Anaphora: Testing the Epistemic Restriction.</em> Poster presented at Experiments in Linguistic Meaning 4 (ELM 4). <span class="paper-links">[<a href="https://elm-conference.net/wp-content/uploads/ELM4/Abstracts/paper144.pdf" target="_blank">abstract</a>]</span>
</span>
</div>

:::

::: {.research-topic .topic-experimental}

## Experimental work

I use experimental evidence to evaluate formal claims about modality and ellipsis. My current work includes an acceptability-judgment experiment with 213 participants testing the proposed epistemic restriction on French modal ellipsis and anaphora.
